---
title: "Creating the ``r params$package_name`` R package"
author: "Sheng Jiang"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
knit: litr::render
output: litr::litr_gitbook
params:
  package_name: "bayesdir" # <-- change this to your package name
  package_parent_dir: "." # <-- relative to this file's location
documentclass: book
---

# Introduction

This package implements `bayesdir`, 
Bayesian directional data analysis, including model 1, model 2. 
The documentation and
package are both created using one simple command:

```{r render, eval = FALSE}
litr::render("index.Rmd", output_format = litr::litr_gitbook())
## install.packages("./bayesdir/", repos = NULL, type="source") 
```

This line is purely for testing (to be deleted later!).

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# Complete development workflow
my_load <- function(){
  litr::render("~/repos/bayesdir/index.Rmd",  
               output_format = litr::litr_gitbook(minimal_eval = TRUE))
  devtools::document("~/repos/bayesdir/bayesdir")  # Update documentation
  devtools::load_all("~/repos/bayesdir/bayesdir")
}

my_load()

# Step 2: Check the package (optional but recommended)
devtools::check("~/repos/bayesdir/bayesdir", document = FALSE)

# Step 3: Install the package locally for full installation
devtools::install("~/repos/bayesdir/bayesdir")

# Step 4: Load the installed package (optional, if you want to test installation)
library(bayesdir)


```


```{r, eval = FALSE}
setwd("~/repos/bayesdir/") 
## 1. Delete the auto-generated _main.Rmd file
unlink("_main.Rmd")
unlink("_bookdown_files", recursive = TRUE)

if(!dir.create("bayesdir")){
  unlink("bayesdir", recursive = TRUE)
}

## 2. Re-run the render command
litr::render(
  "index.Rmd", 
  output_format = litr::litr_gitbook(
    minimal_eval = TRUE 
    )
)

## 3. Document and load the package
devtools::document("bayesdir")
devtools::load_all("bayesdir")
```
